name: Dryslwyn Castle & Tywi Floodplain
terrain: Field path, steep short climb to castle
highlights: Romantic hilltop ruin of a native Welsh castle among the Tywi's languid ox-bows.
points_of_interest: Dryslwyn Castle (Cadw, native Welsh 13th c.); oxbow lakes
viewpoints: Castle summit 360°: Paxton's Tower, Dinefwr, Black Mountain
food_drink_nearby: Y Polyn (at Capel Dewi); Plough Inn Felingwm
hazards_notes: Short but steep final climb; grass slippery when wet
